Name: Terrapin Rocket Team (TerpRockets)
Relevance: Designs and builds high-power rockets for Spaceport America Cup and IREC; has subsystems for propulsion, avionics, and structures.

Name: Satellite Development Program (SatDev)
Relevance: UMD's student-run spaceflight program; long-term goal to launch multiple CubeSats to orbit by 2030, all designed and built by students.

Name: Near Space Balloon Payload Program (BPP)
Relevance: Flies high-altitude balloons to >100k ft with student-designed scientific and engineering payloads; conducts 3-4 launches per semester.

Name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space - UMD (SEDS@Maryland)
Relevance: Project-based UMD chapter of the national SEDS organization; focuses on space mission design, analysis, and testing.

Name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics - UMD (AIAA)
Relevance: One of the largest and most active AIAA student branches on the East Coast; hosts aerospace speakers, field trips, and group projects.

Name: Women in Aeronautics and Astronautics (WIAA)
Relevance: Fosters a professional and inclusive community for students in aeronautics and astronautics; supports technical and professional development and advocacy.

Name: Sigma Gamma Tau - UMD
Relevance: National aerospace engineering honor society chapter; recognizes excellence in the aerospace field among students, alumni, and professionals.

Name: AstroTerps (Terrapin Astronomical Society)
Relevance: Undergraduate astronomy club affiliated with UMD's Department of Astronomy; runs outreach, observing sessions, and community events.

Name: Astronomy Community Engagement (ACE)
Relevance: Student-led group within the UMD Department of Astronomy that facilitates community and access among undergraduate and graduate astronomy students.

Name: Graduate Resources Advancing Diversity with Maryland Astronomy and Physics (GRAD-MAP)
Relevance: Graduate-level diversity program in UMD Astronomy and Physics; recruits and supports students in space science fields.

Name: Robotics @ Maryland
Relevance: Student-run robotics team competing in AUVSI and ONR's RoboSub competition; sponsored by the Maryland Robotics Center with aerospace-adjacent autonomous systems work.

Name: UMD Autonomous Micro Air Vehicle (AMAV) Team
Relevance: Designs builds and flies multirotor VTOL aircraft for the VFS Design-Build-Vertical Flight competition; sponsored by the Maryland Robotics Center.

Name: Maryland Unmanned Aerial Systems Team (MUAS)
Relevance: Builds fixed-wing UAVs for the AUVSI Student UAS Competition; performs autonomous missions with obstacle avoidance and payload delivery.

Name: Turbo Terps
Relevance: Builds autonomous quadrotor UAVs for the IROS UAV Racing Competition; sponsored by the Maryland Robotics Center.

Name: UMD Drone Racing Team
Relevance: Participates in the MultiGP Global Drone Racing League and the Collegiate Drone Racing Association with student-built racing drones.

Name: Space Systems Laboratory (SSL)
Relevance: Centers on a unique 50-ft diameter neutral buoyancy tank simulating microgravity; conducts research in space robotics, EVA simulation, and human factors for spaceflight.

Name: Space Power and Propulsion Laboratory (SPPL)
Relevance: Dedicated to power generation, conversion, and propulsion technologies for space exploration; uses plasma sources and diagnostic tools for electric propulsion research.

Name: Advanced Propulsion Research Laboratory (APRL)
Relevance: Conducts fundamental and applied research on high-speed combustion for gas turbines, ramjets, scramjets, rockets, and detonation engines.

Name: Planetary Surfaces and Spacecraft Lab (PSSL)
Relevance: Computational and experimental lab at the intersection of planetary science, plasma physics, granular mechanics, and spacecraft design.

Name: Strategic Space Sensing Lab
Relevance: Develops advanced sensing methodologies to discover, track, and characterize space objects for space situational awareness, planetary defense, and exploration.

Name: Machine Learning for Dynamical Systems Lab
Relevance: Investigates AI and astrodynamics at their intersection; designs ML models for next-generation spacecraft autonomy in complex dynamical environments.

Name: High-Speed Aerodynamics and Propulsion Laboratory (HAPL)
Relevance: Specializes in hypersonic boundary-layer transition, scramjet unstart, and shock-wave interactions; directly relevant to hypersonic vehicle and propulsion design.

Name: Alfred Gessow Rotorcraft Center (AGRC)
Relevance: One of only three Army/Navy/NASA-designated Vertical Lift Centers of Excellence in the US; conducts multidisciplinary rotorcraft research with direct aerospace-industry applications.

Name: Maryland Robotics Center
Relevance: Interdisciplinary center with 26+ labs and 47 faculty; sponsors multiple student UAV and autonomous vehicle teams with space-adjacent autonomous systems research.

Name: UAS Research and Operations Center (UROC)
Relevance: Leads UMD's FAA-designated UAS test site in St. Mary's County; at the forefront of UAS rulemaking, commercialization, and national airspace integration.

Name: Fearless Flight Facility (F3)
Relevance: Only university outdoor UAS flight lab in the DC-Maryland-Virginia region; serves as a nexus for testing UAS vehicles tied to the UROC test site.

Name: Institute for Research in Electronics and Applied Physics (IREAP)
Relevance: Joint CMNS/Engineering institute conducting research in high-temperature plasma physics, plasma spectroscopy, laser-plasma interactions, and charged particle beams; ranked 3rd nationally in plasma physics graduate programs.

Name: Center for Research and Exploration in Space Science & Technology II (CRESST II)
Relevance: Joint UMD-NASA Goddard center employing ~100 scientists on space science projects spanning astrophysics and solar system exploration; major conduit for student research at GSFC.

Name: Joint Space-Science Institute (JSI)
Relevance: UMD Astronomy/Physics and NASA Goddard partnership focused on black hole physics, high-energy astrophysics, plasma in accretion flows, and cosmology.

Name: Center for Theory and Computation (CTC)
Relevance: Umbrella program within UMD Astronomy for theoretical and computational astrophysics; maintains dedicated compute clusters for large-scale simulations.

Name: Partnership for Heliophysics and Space Environment Research - UMD node (PHaSER)
Relevance: UMD College Park is a member of this $64M NASA-funded consortium supporting Goddard's Heliophysics Science Division; covers solar wind, space weather, and Earth magnetosphere.

Name: Small Bodies Node - NASA Planetary Data System
Relevance: Hosted by UMD Department of Astronomy; serves as NASA's primary archiving and distribution hub for scientific data on asteroids, comets, and interplanetary dust.

Name: Earth System Science Interdisciplinary Center (ESSIC)
Relevance: Joint UMD-NASA Goddard center with a $95M cooperative agreement; ~200 scientists conducting Earth observation and satellite remote sensing research.

Name: Cooperative Institute for Satellite Earth System Studies (CISESS)
Relevance: Administered by ESSIC; joint UMD-NOAA center conducting satellite research on weather and water forecasting with direct satellite instrument work.

Name: Space and Plasma Physics Group (SPP) - Astronomy
Relevance: Studies space plasmas from high-altitude lightning to supernovae shocks; major effort on solar-terrestrial physics with NASA/NSF/AFOSR funding and instruments on Earth-orbiting satellites.

Name: Space Physics Group - Physics
Relevance: Designs and builds plasma and energetic particle instruments on satellites and deep-space probes; research focuses on in-situ space environment measurements.

Name: Theoretical Plasma Group - Physics / IREAP
Relevance: Studies complex nonlinear plasma behavior; closely tied to astrophysical plasma phenomena and space-relevant electromagnetic processes.

Name: Extreme Astrophysical Plasmas Group
Relevance: Theoretical astrophysics group studying relativistic plasmas around neutron stars and black holes; node of NSF's Multi-Messenger Plasma Physics Center (MPPC).

Name: Geospatial Information Science & Remote Sensing Group - Geographical Sciences
Relevance: Develops and applies advanced remote sensing capabilities including satellite image analysis, sensor calibration and design, and geocomputing.

Name: Center for Geospatial Information Science (CGIS)
Relevance: Departmental center offering graduate programs in GIS and remote sensing; research links directly to satellite data processing and Earth observation applications.

Name: International Center for Innovation in Geospatial Analytics & Earth Observation
Relevance: UMD Geographical Sciences center focused on geospatial analytics and Earth observation technology development.

Name: Department of Astronomy - UMD
Relevance: Full research department covering high-energy astrophysics, planetary science, solar/space plasma physics, and cosmology; operates an on-campus observatory and has deep ties to NASA Goddard.

Name: Department of Aerospace Engineering - UMD
Relevance: Offers specializations in space systems, propulsion, hypersonics, dynamics/control, and rotorcraft; houses most of the space-facing research labs listed in this table.

Name: UMD Observatory
Relevance: On-campus observatory with four fixed telescopes (7-20 inch aperture) and portable 8-inch scopes used for undergraduate research, courses, and public outreach.

Name: UMDLoop / Maryland Boring
Relevance: Interdisciplinary student team competing in Elon Musk's Hyperloop and Not-a-Boring competitions; hyperloop propulsion and guidance systems overlap with aerospace/avionics.

Name: Collective Dynamics and Control Laboratory (CDCL)
Relevance: Research in multi-vehicle control and autonomous vehicles; bio-inspired collective behavior applied to autonomous aerial and aquatic robots - space robotics adjacent.

Name: Society of Physics Students - UMD
Relevance: Physics undergraduate club; members frequently engage with astrophysics and space science seminars through the department's strong space-physics presence.

Name: Glenn L. Martin Wind Tunnel (GLMWT)
Relevance: Subsonic wind tunnel operating since 1949; used for aerodynamic testing of aircraft and UAV models - adjacent infrastructure for aerospace propulsion and design.

Name: Composites Research Laboratory (CORE)
Relevance: Research in composite materials and structures relevant to aerospace vehicle construction including lightweight structural components used in rockets and spacecraft.

Name: Film Cooling Research Laboratory
Relevance: Studies film cooling physics for gas turbine and rocket engine components; directly supports thermal management in high-speed propulsion systems.

Name: Maryland Center for Fundamental Physics (MCFP)
Relevance: Houses particle and nuclear physics research; overlaps with space physics through dark matter searches and high-energy particle astrophysics.

Name: Institute for Physical Science and Technology (IPST)
Relevance: Historically housed a major plasma physics group; current research spans fluid dynamics and applied physics with some overlap with space and plasma research.
